title: Reusability with the `children` Prop

learningObjectives.text: Apply composition slots using a new example and justify the result.
Explain reusable wrappers using a new example and justify the result.
Explain flexible component content using a new example and justify the result.

whyThisMatters: Reusability with the `children` Prop is part of the mental model learners need before making reliable implementation decisions. It belongs to the **Component Design and Reusability** module.
mentalModel: Draw a decision map: **Composition slots** → **Reusable wrappers** → **Flexible component content**. Add branches where the learner must choose an alternative and label the evidence for the choice.

sections.title: Composition slots
sections.body: The children prop is a composition slot: callers provide nested content while a wrapper controls stable layout or behavior.

sections.title: Reusable wrappers
sections.body: A reusable wrapper should centralize meaningful layout, accessibility, or behavior rather than merely move markup to another file.

sections.title: Flexible component content
sections.body: Use children for the primary nested region and named element props when multiple distinct content regions need clear contracts.

sections.title: Worked example
sections.explanation: The wrapper owns layout while callers provide flexible nested content.

commonMistakes: Mutating props or state instead of producing the next value.
Adding state or an Effect for a value that can be derived during render.
Using global state before identifying the real owner and consumers.
Optimizing renders without a measured user-facing problem.
summary: Reusability with the `children` Prop connects Composition slots, Reusable wrappers, Flexible component content.
Use the topic to make behavior and decisions explicit rather than memorizing isolated syntax.
Verify understanding by explaining a new example and answering the topic quiz without looking back.

topicQuiz.failurePolicy: Show explanations, add missed objectives to local review, and allow immediate or later retry.

assessmentProfile.label: Code-reading, debugging, and implementation challenge
assessmentProfile.rationale: The topic represents a day-to-day coding skill. The topic quiz must primarily test code reading, debugging, output prediction, or implementation choice.
assessmentProfile.inLessonPolicy: Use concise concept checks; coding and hybrid topics include at least one code-reading check.
assessmentProfile.topicQuizPolicy: Questions Q04–Q08 form the topic quiz. The presentation mix follows this profile.
